Details:
Inactivation of Cryptosporidium oocysts by shaking with sand was found to be very effective for one study (>99% of oocysts were killed after shaking with sand for 90 minutes). The study also found increased inactivation of Cryptosporidium with chlorine concentrations typically used in water treatment after shaking oocysts with sand for five minutes.
Removal of Cryptosporidium in water by a Fribrotex AX50 pilot plant was found to be effective for one study (98 to 99.1% oocyst removal) when treating a groundwater source under the influence of surface water.
